integrations.implementationNotes: System prompt sent as plain string in anthropicApi.ts. Needs content block array with cache_control: { type: 'ephemeral' } on evidence blocks. Gossamer and Inquiry send identical evidence across calls — caching would save ~90% on input tokens.
blockers: Requires anthropic-system-content-blocks to be implemented first

integrations.implementationNotes: callAnthropicApi sends system as a plain string (line 47: requestBody.system = systemPrompt). Must restructure to array of content blocks: [{ type: 'text', text: ... }]. Prerequisite for prompt caching.

callAnthropicApi does not accept or send temperature/topP. The providerCapabilities sanitizer strips these for Anthropic. Gossamer temperature of 0.45 and Inquiry temperature of 0.2 are silently dropped when using Anthropic.
Could improve analysis quality for complex Inquiry questions and Gossamer beat analysis by allowing Claude to reason through evidence before answering.

Current token estimation uses chars/4 heuristic (estimateTokensFromVault.ts). Anthropic's count_tokens endpoint provides exact counts. Would improve the 80% input guard accuracy in aiClient.ts line 350.
Inquiry findings could include source citations linking back to specific scenes/documents. Would strengthen evidence attribution in InquiryResult findings.

Gossamer currently runs a single unified API call. Batch API would help if architecture changed to per-beat analysis, or for bulk processing of multiple manuscripts. 50% cost reduction but 24h turnaround.
blockers: Current architecture uses single unified call, not per-beat

integrations.implementationNotes: Would enable real-time progress feedback during long AI calls. Currently users see a spinner with no intermediate results.
blockers: Obsidian requestUrl() does not support SSE streaming. Would need browser fetch() with ReadableStream, which may not work on mobile Obsidian.

integrations.implementationNotes: Plugin works with Obsidian markdown vault files, not PDFs. Manuscript content is already available as text.

OpenAI automatically caches prompts >1024 tokens with stable prefixes (50% discount). Currently DEFEATED because openaiApi.ts line 82 concatenates system+user into a single user message. Fix: separate into system and user messages for non-reasoning models.
Implemented via response_format: { type: 'json_schema', json_schema: { name: 'ai_result', schema: req.jsonSchema } } in openaiProvider.ts generateJson method.

openaiApi.ts concatenates all prompts into a single user message 'because reasoning models don't support system role'. This is over-broad — only o1/o1-mini lack system role support. GPT-5.x and later o-series models support system/developer roles. Need precise model detection.
Similar to Anthropic batch API. 50% cost reduction for async processing. Same architectural constraint: Gossamer currently uses a single unified call.
blockers: Current architecture uses single unified call, not per-beat

integrations.implementationNotes: Same blocker as Anthropic streaming. Would enable progress feedback.
blockers: Obsidian requestUrl() does not support SSE streaming.

integrations.implementationNotes: New API replacing chat completions for some use cases. Still in preview. Current chat completions API is stable and sufficient.
blockers: No clear benefit over chat completions for plugin's use cases

integrations.implementationNotes: createGeminiCache() exists at geminiApi.ts:186 and callGeminiApi() accepts cachedContentName at param 9. However, NO CALLERS wire these together. Need a GeminiCacheManager that creates cache resources and threads the cache name through the provider.
Gemini models configured with contextWindow: 1048576 in builtinModels.ts. Full manuscript fits in context.
Implemented via responseMimeType: 'application/json' and responseSchema in geminiApi.ts generationConfig.
Same Obsidian requestUrl blocker as other providers.
blockers: Obsidian requestUrl() does not support SSE streaming.

integrations.implementationNotes: Search grounding is useful for factual queries but not for manuscript analysis. Plugin's analysis is based on vault content, not web search.
